title: Accounts Receivable Aging and Cash Collection
dataSources: Primary: `index=business` with Splunk DB Connect–fed JDBC rows using sourcetype `dbx:ar_invoices` (deployment-defined name on the DB Connect Data Input). Each event should expose coalesce-friendly invoice keys and ERP-native aliases: `invoice_id`, `BELNR`, `vbeln`, `document_number`, `transaction_id`; customer `customer_id` / `KUNNR` / `customer` / NetSuite-style `entityid`; open balance `amount` / `amount_due_remaining` / SAP `DMBTR` or `WRBTR` when your SQL projects FI-AR line items; **currency** as `currency`, `WAERS`, or ISO `currency_iso` (functional-currency normalization belongs in SQL or a treasury macro—do not mix FX without a rate table); due clock inputs `due_date`, SAP baseline `ZFBDT`, Oracle `due_date`, NetSuite `duedate`; optional `invoice_date`, `BLDAT`, `BUDAT`, `trandate`; status `status` / `fi_doc_status`; `dispute_flag`; `factoring_status` (`NONE` vs factored); `customer_segment` or a mappable tier field; optional `aging_as_of_date` when you snapshot “as of close” instead of live `now()`. Companion feed for DSO denominator: `index=business` `sourcetype="dbx:finance_revenue_daily"` (or an equivalent rolling credit-sales aggregate your controller signs off), field `credit_sales_amount` by company code. Lookups: optional `customer_segment_map.csv` if segments are maintained outside the JDBC projection.
app: Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)), package `splunk_app_db_connect`, installed on a Heavy Forwarder or supported DB Connect server. JDBC source must be authorized read-only SQL over the finance schema your controller treats as subledger truth—examples: SAP S/4HANA or ECC with FI-AR line items exposed from **BSID** (open items) versus **BSAD** (cleared history) joined to customer masters **KNA1**/**KNB1** for payment terms **ZTERM**; Oracle EBS **AR_PAYMENT_SCHEDULES_ALL** with **HZ_PARTIES**; NetSuite SuiteAnalytics **Transaction** rows for **CustInvc**; Microsoft Dynamics 365 Finance **CustTransOpen** projections; Workday Financials receivable facts replicated to a warehouse. No Splunkbase TA ships canonical AR table bodies—you own the SQL view and sourcetype naming.
spl:
index=business sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
| eval invoice_key=tostring(coalesce(invoice_id,BELNR,vbeln,document_number,transaction_id,""))
| eval cust_key=tostring(coalesce(customer_id,KUNNR,customer,customer_entity,"UNKNOWN"))
| eval amt_fc=tonumber(coalesce(amount,amount_due_remaining,DMBTR,WRBTR,amountremaining,"0"))
| eval curr=tostring(coalesce(currency,WAERS,currency_iso,"USD"))
| eval due_raw=coalesce(due_date,duedate,ZFBDT,ZFBDT_due)
| eval due_epoch=coalesce(strptime(due_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"),strptime(due_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"))
| eval due_epoch=if(isnull(due_epoch),strptime(coalesce(invoice_date,BLDAT,BUDAT,trandate),"%Y-%m-%d"),due_epoch)
| eval clock=if(isnotnull(strptime(aging_as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")),strptime(aging_as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),now())
| eval days_overdue=if(isnull(due_epoch),null(),round((clock-due_epoch)/86400,2))
| eval status_norm=lower(tostring(coalesce(status,ar_status,"open")))
| where status_norm IN ("open","op","posted","partial","past_due","past due") OR status_norm="" OR isnull(status_norm)
| eval fs=upper(tostring(coalesce(factoring_status,"NONE")))
| where fs IN ("NONE","NOT_FACTORED","UNFACTORED","") OR isnull(factoring_status)
| eval aging_bucket=case(isnull(days_overdue) OR days_overdue<=0,"Current",days_overdue<=30,"1-30",days_overdue<=60,"31-60",days_overdue<=90,"61-90",days_overdue<=120,"91-120",1=1,"120+")
| eval cs_raw=lower(tostring(coalesce(customer_segment,cust_seg,"midmarket")))
| eval cust_segment=case(in(cs_raw,"enterprise","strategic","ent","global"),"enterprise",in(cs_raw,"smb","small","sm"),"smb",in(cs_raw,"midmarket","mid","mm"),"midmarket",1=1,"midmarket")
| eval pd=case(aging_bucket=="Current",0.5,aging_bucket=="1-30",2,aging_bucket=="31-60",5,aging_bucket=="61-90",15,aging_bucket=="91-120",40,1=1,85)
| eval ecl_amt=round(amt_fc*pd/100,2)
| eval ecl_amt=if(cust_segment=="enterprise",round(ecl_amt*1.15,2),if(cust_segment=="smb",round(ecl_amt*0.85,2),ecl_amt))
| stats sum(amt_fc) as total_outstanding sum(ecl_amt) as bucket_ecl dc(invoice_key) as inv_ct dc(cust_key) as cust_ct by aging_bucket cust_segment curr
| eventstats sum(total_outstanding) as portfolio_total
| eval pct_of_total=round(100*total_outstanding/nullif(portfolio_total,0),2)
| join max=1 type=left [
  search index=business sourcetype="dbx:finance_revenue_daily" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
  | stats sum(credit_sales_amount) as total_credit_sales
 ]
| eval dso_days=round((portfolio_total/nullif(total_credit_sales,0))*90,2)
| sort aging_bucket cust_segment curr
| table aging_bucket cust_segment curr total_outstanding pct_of_total inv_ct cust_ct bucket_ecl dso_days total_credit_sales
description: Rolls up open accounts-receivable lines ingested as `dbx:ar_invoices` into standard aging buckets (Current through 120+ days past due), after coalescing cross-ERP invoice and customer keys, computing `days_overdue` from the due-date epoch against an as-of clock, filtering factored receivables out of collection priority, layering customer-segment labels, and attaching a CECL/IFRS-9 style expected credit loss estimate from bucket-level PD×LGD-style percentages with segment multipliers—then exposes portfolio percentage, counts, and a DSO-style readout joined to a trailing credit-sales denominator from `dbx:finance_revenue_daily`.
value: Treasury, collections, and the office of the CFO see the same overdue concentration, segment mix, and implied reserve pressure that board packs and covenant monitoring use—without waiting for month-end BI refresh. Cash-application and dispute teams can prioritize which customers and buckets to chase first when working capital tightens, while risk and control partners get a defendable tie from operational AR ledgers toward allowance models discussed under CECL and IFRS 9, even though policy still lives in accounting policy manuals, not Splunk.
implementation: (1) Publish a JDBC DB Connect batch or rising input that projects only open FI-AR candidates (SAP **BSID**-style open items, Oracle schedules with status OP, NetSuite open invoice balances) into `index=business` as `dbx:ar_invoices`, with `interval = 3600` or a batch window aligned to ERP load. (2) Land a parallel `dbx:finance_revenue_daily` (or ledger credit-sales aggregate) with `credit_sales_amount` the CFO accepts as the DSO denominator. (3) Optional: add `customer_segment_map.csv` if segment is not on the invoice row. (4) Save the SPL as a daily leadership panel and a collections work-queue filtered to `aging_bucket` != Current. (5) Route high `120+` concentration with rising `pct_of_total` to the CRO and treasury; keep model tuning (PD/LGD tables) under corporate accounting policy.
visualization: Dashboard Studio: trellised stacked columns of `total_outstanding` by `aging_bucket` and `cust_segment`; single value for `dso_days` with prior-period token; heat table of `pct_of_total` by bucket; detail drill table (`cust_key`, `curr`, oldest `days_overdue`, `ecl_amt`); line trend of `portfolio_total` and `dso_days` over week slices from saved summary.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Deploy and use DB Connect
SAP Help — Accounts Receivable line items (extract context: BSID open items, BSAD cleared items)
Oracle Cloud AR — Payment schedules and applications (AR_PAYMENT_SCHEDULES_ALL context)
FASB Accounting Standards Update ASU 2016-13 — Measurement of Credit Losses on Financial Instruments (CECL)

knownFalsePositives: Cash applied in the bank on T but posted to FI on T+2 frequently produces a short phantom overdue spike at month boundaries until the clearing batch mirrors treasury reality—indexed rows still show open AR though collectors already hold remittance advice. Invoices parked fully in SAP **FSCM-DM** dispute cases while the FI line remains technically open inflate overdue dollars versus collection priority until dispute resolution closes or writes down amounts. Intercompany billing remains aged in a subsidiary ledger until elimination posts even though consolidation nets to zero group-wide. Credit memos issued against invoices before cancellation postings settle can swing buckets negatively until netting completes. Without-recourse factoring removes balances upstream; with-recourse factoring leaves balances flagged—mis-tagged `factoring_status` sends collectors toward invoices the factor already owns. Quarter-end **F.05** foreign-currency valuation runs shift translated balances without customer payments changing when functional currency differs from transaction currency. ERP nightly re-aging jobs started between 02:00–04:00 UTC often leave Monday morning dashboards one refresh behind treasury spreadsheets until Finance confirms weekend receipt postings finished. Statistical sampling programmes that randomly delay cash-application QA batches near audit windows can widen Splunk-versus-bank variance without any customer paying late. Deferred-revenue schedules that park contra balances beside gross AR sometimes resemble overdue invoices until accountants move amounts into contract-liability accounts—nothing here diagnoses revenue timing misclassification alone. Rolling ninety-day revenue denominators that include heavy returns during recall programmes shrink apparent DSO versus operational expectation until FP&A revises the credit-sales definition feeding `dbx:finance_revenue_daily`. Consolidation-only netting entries booked after subsidiary snapshots replicate zero cash risk at group level yet leave operational dashboards flashing tier-two buckets until Corporate posts eliminations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier aligned with splunkVersions; Heavy Forwarder or dedicated Splunk DB Connect worker with JDBC paths into SAP HANA (`jdbc:sap://host:3NN15`), Oracle (SCAN listener), SQL Server finance warehouses, or NetSuite SuiteAnalytics Connect—firewall approvals documented by ticket.
• Splunk DB Connect identities holding least-privilege read credentials against curated views (`v_ar_open_invoices`) rather than carte-blanche SELECT on production ERP pools.
• Corporate Accounting policy covering allowance percentages—Splunk illustrates CECL/IFRS-style mathematics for operational prioritization; auditors still rely on governance memos.
• Splunk roles restricting `index=business` AR detail to treasury, collections, and designated FP&A personas.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Install Splunk DB Connect from Splunkbase 2686 on the Heavy Forwarder; load JDBC drivers (`ngdbc.jar`, `ojdbc*.jar`). Under Connections, bind SAP example URL `jdbc:sap://<hana-host>:30015/?databaseName=<SID>` with user scoped to CDS/SQL views exposing open FI-AR analogues to **BSID** (`BUKRS`, `KUNNR`, `UMSKS`, `DMBTR`, `WRBTR`, `WAERS`, `ZFBDT`, `BUDAT`) filtered `AUGDT IS NULL`; Oracle teams expose **AR_PAYMENT_SCHEDULES_ALL** with `amount_due_remaining`, `due_date`, `status`; NetSuite emits SuiteAnalytics columns `{tranid, trandate, duedate, amountremaining, status, currency}`. Emit hourly batch pulls matching representative inputs.conf semantics: `interval = 3600`, `mode = batch`, query predicates such as `WHERE last_updated > ?` using Splunk lookahead macros. Name the sourcetype `dbx:ar_invoices` into `index=business`; map `_time` from `snapshot_timestamp` or `last_updated` so dashboards reconcile to ERP snapshots rather than indexer latency alone.

Stand up a second DB Connect input (or reuse ledger mart SQL) landing `dbx:finance_revenue_daily` rows with `credit_sales_amount` summed to the same reporting currency policy treasury publishes for covenant worksheets—typically trailing ninety calendar days at daily grain so the join supplies a coherent DSO denominator without double-counting returns.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the SPL shown in the top-level `spl` field into Search, adjust `earliest/latest` windows, then save as a scheduled report (`ar_aging_cash_collection_daily`) plus an optional alert when `pct_of_total` for bucket `120+` exceeds policy for two consecutive snapshots. Throttle alerts by `aging_bucket` and `cust_segment` tuple to reduce noise during known close windows.

```spl
index=business sourcetype="dbx:ar_invoices" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
| eval invoice_key=tostring(coalesce(invoice_id,BELNR,vbeln,document_number,transaction_id,""))
| eval cust_key=tostring(coalesce(customer_id,KUNNR,customer,customer_entity,"UNKNOWN"))
| eval amt_fc=tonumber(coalesce(amount,amount_due_remaining,DMBTR,WRBTR,amountremaining,"0"))
| eval curr=tostring(coalesce(currency,WAERS,currency_iso,"USD"))
| eval due_raw=coalesce(due_date,duedate,ZFBDT,ZFBDT_due)
| eval due_epoch=coalesce(strptime(due_raw,"%Y-%m-%d"),strptime(due_raw,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3Q"))
| eval due_epoch=if(isnull(due_epoch),strptime(coalesce(invoice_date,BLDAT,BUDAT,trandate),"%Y-%m-%d"),due_epoch)
| eval clock=if(isnotnull(strptime(aging_as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")),strptime(aging_as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),now())
| eval days_overdue=if(isnull(due_epoch),null(),round((clock-due_epoch)/86400,2))
| eval status_norm=lower(tostring(coalesce(status,ar_status,"open")))
| where status_norm IN ("open","op","posted","partial","past_due","past due") OR status_norm="" OR isnull(status_norm)
| eval fs=upper(tostring(coalesce(factoring_status,"NONE")))
| where fs IN ("NONE","NOT_FACTORED","UNFACTORED","") OR isnull(factoring_status)
| eval aging_bucket=case(isnull(days_overdue) OR days_overdue<=0,"Current",days_overdue<=30,"1-30",days_overdue<=60,"31-60",days_overdue<=90,"61-90",days_overdue<=120,"91-120",1=1,"120+")
| eval cs_raw=lower(tostring(coalesce(customer_segment,cust_seg,"midmarket")))
| eval cust_segment=case(in(cs_raw,"enterprise","strategic","ent","global"),"enterprise",in(cs_raw,"smb","small","sm"),"smb",in(cs_raw,"midmarket","mid","mm"),"midmarket",1=1,"midmarket")
| eval pd=case(aging_bucket=="Current",0.5,aging_bucket=="1-30",2,aging_bucket=="31-60",5,aging_bucket=="61-90",15,aging_bucket=="91-120",40,1=1,85)
| eval ecl_amt=round(amt_fc*pd/100,2)
| eval ecl_amt=if(cust_segment=="enterprise",round(ecl_amt*1.15,2),if(cust_segment=="smb",round(ecl_amt*0.85,2),ecl_amt))
| stats sum(amt_fc) as total_outstanding sum(ecl_amt) as bucket_ecl dc(invoice_key) as inv_ct dc(cust_key) as cust_ct by aging_bucket cust_segment curr
| eventstats sum(total_outstanding) as portfolio_total
| eval pct_of_total=round(100*total_outstanding/nullif(portfolio_total,0),2)
| join max=1 type=left [
  search index=business sourcetype="dbx:finance_revenue_daily" earliest=-90d@d latest=@d
  | stats sum(credit_sales_amount) as total_credit_sales
 ]
| eval dso_days=round((portfolio_total/nullif(total_credit_sales,0))*90,2)
| sort aging_bucket cust_segment curr
| table aging_bucket cust_segment curr total_outstanding pct_of_total inv_ct cust_ct bucket_ecl dso_days total_credit_sales
```

Step 3 — Pipeline mechanics
The opening predicate binds JDBC-ingested AR snapshots into `index=business` so Splunk timelines reflect ERP clocks carried on `_time`, while `latest=@d` trims intra-day noise during reconciliation drills. Invoice identifiers collapse through `coalesce()` across SAP (`BELNR`), Salesforce naming (`transaction_id`), and generic ledger exports (`document_number`) so one SPL survives ERP migrations without rewriting forty dashboards.

`due_epoch` prefers textual due dates but falls back to invoice posting dates when downstream warehouses omit baseline dates—finance must accept that fallback buckets invoices conservatively toward overdue until SQL fixes land.

`clock` swaps between literal `now()` and optional `aging_as_of_date` when teams freeze dashboards during quarter-end comparisons across treasury spreadsheets versus Splunk snapshots.

`days_overdue` divides elapsed seconds by eighty-six thousand four hundred for human-readable floats suitable for downstream anomaly scoring.

`aging_bucket` applies inclusive ladder thresholds aligned to collections industry norms—extend only after aligning with corporate credit policy.

Customer tiers consolidate synonyms (`ent`, `strategic`) before CECL-style percentages multiply balances—explicitly not statutory reserving until Accounting publishes authoritative schedules.

`stats … by aging_bucket cust_segment curr` emits rolled balances suitable for mixed-currency portfolios only after upstream SQL translates into functional currency; Splunk warns when raw rows carry mixed currencies without normalization.

`eventstats sum(total_outstanding)` calculates portfolio totals replicated across bucket rows powering `pct_of_total` concentration diagnostics executives watch during covenant conversations.

`join` retrieves trailing credit sales feeding `dso_days`; null denominators yield empty `dso_days`, signalling upstream gaps rather than silently inventing revenue.

Step 4 — Validate
Compare JDBC-derived totals against vendor interfaces—not Splunk alone—before escalating working-capital narratives: pull five `invoice_key` values flagged in `120+`, locate each in SAP transaction **FBL5N** / **FD11**, Oracle **Receivables Balances** inquiry pages, NetSuite Transactions → Invoice, or Dynamics **Collections workspace** dashboards depending on landscape—confirm due amounts match ±rounding tolerance against JDBC decimals exported from ERP numeric types.

Weekly, compare against the SAP collections dashboard or Oracle receivables dashboard so Splunk bucket totals tie before executive reviews.

Compare summed `amt_fc` by company code against FI subledger balances Controlling publishes for the same fiscal period—material deviations trace to JDBC filters omitting credit memo netting or clearing docs.

Validate `total_credit_sales` against SAP revenue recognition extracts (`VBRK` net billings versus FI postings) per UC-23.2.2 pairing discipline when SAP underpins revenue.

Confirm `factoring_status` encoding with treasury: invoices with recourse factoring stay on-book for legal ownership but collection rights may sit with the factor—Splunk excludes them only when treasury tags the field.

Compare `dso_days` to the manual DSO workbook Finance emails to lenders; if off by more than two days, reconcile the ninety-day credit-sales window against returns netting rules feeding `credit_sales_amount`.

Step 5 — Operational caveats and known false positives
• Bank deposits that clear overnight but post to FI two to five days later inflate overdue counts on the first business day of a month—correlate with lockbox batch IDs before chasing customers.
• Disputed lines that stay open in FI while dispute management owns the negotiation show as overdue even when legal hold pauses collections—join optional `dispute_case_id` feeds or mark `dispute_flag=Y` rows for a separate work stream.
• Intercompany AR that nets in group reporting but ages in entity books will overstate external bad-debt signals unless you filter IC `customer_id` sets from customer-facing dashboards.
• Credit memo issuance lagging invoice reversals temporarily shows negative balances in raw data; aggregation by absolute value is wrong—enforce sign rules in SQL before indexing.
• With-recourse factoring leaves receivables recorded while cash collection rights transfer; missing `factoring_status` tagging points collectors at the wrong accounts receivable balances.
• ERP period cutover batches that re-age at 02:00–04:00 local can desynchronize from manual Monday morning Excel until the nightly job finishes—prefer `aging_as_of_date` snapshots tied to close books.
• Multi-currency environments where `F.05` revaluation posts new ledger entries without cash movement swing `amt_fc` while `days_overdue` is unchanged—split FCY versus reporting-currency panels when treasury requires it.
• Master-data changes extending terms from Net thirty to Net sixty mid-quarter without refreshing `due_date` on existing lines create false streaks of pseudo delinquency until data stewards patch history.
• Customer bankruptcy filings that move invoices to litigation or hold status without changing dated due fields keep AR lines in past-due buckets though collection strategy pivoted—monitor legal status feeds separately.
• Milestone billing schedules keyed on contractual invoice dates ahead of revenue recognition events may cluster artificial overdue spikes versus operational shipment timing—Finance distinguishes billing drift through UC-23.2.2 sibling feeds.
• Deferred revenue contra postings miscategorized into AR buckets surface as aged noise until correcting journals realign revenue versus billing.
• JDBC outages replay large overlapping batches inflating `_raw` duplicates—monitor DB Connect connection warnings (`ORA-01017`, SQLSTATE `42501`) and watermark checkpoints before trusting overnight deltas.
• Credential rotations that drift between ERP vault and Splunk DB Connect identities trigger authentication failures until both stores align—watch DB Connect Identity test-connection errors referencing JDBC credential denied responses.
grandmaExplanation: We line up every unpaid customer bill by how late it is versus its due date, add up what share sits in each lateness band, and highlight who owes the most so collectors call the right accounts first instead of guessing from one big total.
